name: Western Union
description_short: Western Union operates 1,850+ agent locations across the US offering in-person money transfers, bill payments, money orders, and cash pickup services since 1851.
description_long:
Western Union is a global money transfer company that has provided financial services since 1851, establishing itself as a trusted name in domestic and international fund transfers. The company operates through a network of hundreds of agent locations across the United States, partnering with major retailers like Walmart, grocery chains, and banks to provide accessible in-person financial services.

Western Union's core offerings include domestic and international money transfers, bill payment services (utilities, rent, car payments), money order issuance and cashing, and cash pickup for recipients. Customers can initiate transfers online or via the mobile app and complete transactions in person at agent locations, or conduct full transactions at the counter. The company provides a Money Transfer Control Number (MTCN) for tracking and recipient pickup verification. They also offer prepaid Visa cards as an alternative to cash pickup and partner with multiple banks and retail locations for service accessibility.

What distinguishes Western Union is its extensive physical footprint and flexibility in transaction initiation—customers can start transfers online to save time or conduct entirely in-person transactions. The company emphasizes fraud prevention and encryption for security, offers first-time international transfer fee waivers online, and maintains varying hours based on host location. They explicitly do not offer currency exchange at agent locations but handle international transfers with real-time exchange rates applied at transfer time.

However, Western Union functions primarily as a transaction facilitator rather than a financial institution—it does not provide savings accounts, credit products, or traditional banking services. Pricing varies significantly by transfer method, payment option, and destination, requiring customers to use their cost calculator to compare fees. The company currently experiences delays on specific payment corridors (noted: Orange Wallet in Cameroon), and service availability depends entirely on host location hours and participation.
pros: Extensive US network with 100+ agent locations in retail stores, grocery chains, and banks for convenient access
Multiple transaction initiation options: online/app pre-registration to save counter time, or full in-person service
Fast cash pickup for recipients at participating locations with MTCN tracking confirmation
Flexible payment methods at select locations including cash, debit, and credit cards
First online international money transfer is fee-free for new customers
Money order services as a trusted alternative to checks for secure cash transfers
Established 170+ year reputation with fraud prevention and encryption security measures
cons: Pricing varies significantly by transfer method and destination; requires manual comparison using their cost calculator
No currency exchange services at physical locations despite international money transfer offerings
Service hours and availability completely dependent on host location (Walmart, bank, etc.)
Currently experiencing delays on certain payment corridors (e.g., Orange Wallet in Cameroon)
International transfers require recipient's full name as it appears on ID and specific location details, adding friction
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers who need fast cash-based money transfers without a bank account
People sending money domestically or internationally to family and friends who prefer cash pickup
Bill payers seeking convenient in-person payment for utilities, rent, and other recurring bills
Consumers who prefer to complete transactions in person with direct agent assistance rather than online
diagnosis: Western Union is best for unbanked consumers and those sending cash-based money transfers domestically or internationally who have reliable access to agent locations during business hours. The primary caveat is that fees and rates vary significantly by transfer type and destination, requiring customers to actively compare costs, and service availability is entirely dependent on host location hours rather than dedicated Western Union operating hours.
services: International in-person money transfers with real-time exchange rates
Online money transfer initiation with in-person cash payment completion
Bill payment services (utilities, rent, car payments) at participating locations
Cash pickup for recipients using MTCN and valid ID
Western Union Prepaid Visa Card for direct fund receipt (no minimum balance or credit check)
Mobile app for transfer tracking and account management
Bank partner services for in-person transactions
Retail location integration at Walmart, grocery chains, and major retailers
Money Transfer Control Number (MTCN) tracking system
Fraud prevention and encryption for transaction security
